title: Tech's massive AI spend is under scrutiny ahead of earnings. Here's what to watch
text: As big tech companies prepare to report quarterly earnings, their massive spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure has come under intense scrutiny. The scale of AI investment is massive. Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft collectively estimate they will invest up to $665 billion in AI this year, almost 75% more than the $381 billion they spent in 2025. Wall Street analysts estimate total AI capital expenditures could climb above $1 trillion in 2027. Key areas of focus include whether cloud margins hold as companies continue to shift resources toward AI. Microsoft reported 40% Azure growth, while Amazon cited strong cloud demand. Google Cloud's operating margin nearly doubled year-over-year, from 17.8% to 32.9%, in a single year. These cloud businesses provide the financial cushion allowing companies to justify massive AI infrastructure investments. Investor concerns center on return on investment and whether the expensive hardware fueling AI will become obsolete at lightning speed. Despite strong earnings, Meta declined 6% after-hours, while Microsoft and Amazon slipped on spending concerns. The market is watching closely for signs that AI investments are translating into concrete revenue opportunities beyond internal efficiency gains.
